flagged_questions.issue: The marked answer is a literal description of the first stanza, but the question asks what the contrast 'signifies'. The contrast between the genuine past and the fake present signifies the broader theme of hypocrisy and pretension in modern human behavior, which is captured in the third option. The first option is an example of the contrast, not the significance of it.
flagged_questions.original_answer: People before laughed with their hearts. Now, their laughter lacks warmth and spirit.
flagged_questions.corrected_answer: Hypocrisy and pretensions are the salient features of human behavior today.
flagged_questions.original_explanation: The opening lines specifically draw the contrast: 'they used to laugh with their hearts... but now they only laugh with their teeth, while their ice-block-cold eyes...' showing a shift from genuine warmth to spirited coldness.
flagged_questions.corrected_explanation: The contrast between the past (laughing/shaking hands with the heart) and the present (laughing with teeth/shaking hands without hearts) signifies the overarching theme of the poem: the prevalence of hypocrisy, superficiality, and pretension in modern society.

flagged_questions.issue: The option 'special rather than general' is a definition of 'particular' (meaning specific), so it should not be the 'EXCEPT' answer. The explanation claims the word does not mean 'special as in exalted', but the option provided is 'special rather than general', which is not the same thing. 'Precise' is the most likely 'EXCEPT' answer as 'particular' here refers to identity/specificity, not accuracy.
flagged_questions.original_explanation: Here, 'particular' refers to his precise, individual, specific personal particles (apart from others) rather than something 'special' as in exalted or superior.
flagged_questions.corrected_explanation: In the phrase 'particular remains', 'particular' means specific, individual, or personal (apart from others). While 'special rather than general' is a synonym for specific, 'precise' typically refers to exactness or accuracy, which is not the intended meaning in this context.

flagged_questions.issue: The claim that the stream represents 'people in a state of innocence' is not supported by the text. The stream is a metaphor for the individual, unified state of the speaker's existence (his body and works) before they are dispersed by death and time.
flagged_questions.original_answer: people in a state of innocence.
flagged_questions.original_explanation: A 'sober stream' often figures as a metaphor for an individual human life or an innocent soul progressing into the vast, mingling 'wilder ocean' of the world or eternity.
flagged_questions.corrected_explanation: The stream represents the speaker's unified existence or identity before it is scattered into the 'wilder ocean' of eternity or death, mirroring how his 'remains' are scattered as grains.

flagged_questions.issue: 'To no purpose' means futile, pointless, or in vain. 'Unresolved' means not settled or not finished, which is not a synonym for futility.
flagged_questions.original_explanation: 'To No Purpose' generally means it is a futile, unresolved act, just as trying to trace the water's path back exactly is pointless and cannot be resolved.
flagged_questions.corrected_explanation: 'To no purpose' is an idiom meaning that an action is useless or will not achieve the desired result (futile).

flagged_questions.issue: The marked answer refers to 'sa ating bansa' (in our country), but the title 'May Plastik ang Mundo' specifically uses the word 'Mundo' (World). Option 3 ('iba't-ibang panig ng daigdig') is a more accurate interpretation of the title's literal and metaphorical scope than Option 2.
flagged_questions.original_answer: laganap ang pagbabalatkayo sa ating bansa.
flagged_questions.corrected_answer: laganap ang mapagkunwari sa iba't-ibang panig ng daigdig.
flagged_questions.original_explanation: Ang pamagat na 'May Plastik ang Mundo' na naka-adres kay 'Huwan' (Juan, na tumutukoy sa lipunang Pilipino) ay nagpapahiwatig ng talamak na pagbabalatkayo (hypocrisy) ng mga tao.
flagged_questions.corrected_explanation: Ang pamagat na 'May Plastik ang Mundo' ay gumagamit ng salitang 'plastik' bilang metapora para sa pagbabalatkayo o pagiging mapagkunwari. Dahil ang salitang ginamit ay 'Mundo', ang ipinahihiwatig nito ay ang paglaganap ng ganitong ugali sa buong daigdig, hindi lamang sa loob ng isang bansa.

corrected_questions.subject: Reading Comprehension
corrected_questions.question: Okara contrasts the past with the present. What does this signify?
options: People before laughed with their hearts. Now, their laughter lacks warmth and spirit.
Once, showing hospitality to guests was a common thing. But these days, no one wants visitors.
Hypocrisy and pretensions are the salient features of human behavior today.
The concept of friendship is not what it used to be.
corrected_questions.correctAnswer: Hypocrisy and pretensions are the salient features of human behavior today.
corrected_questions.explanation: The contrast between the past (laughing/shaking hands with the heart) and the present (laughing with teeth/shaking hands without hearts) signifies the overarching theme of the poem: the prevalence of hypocrisy, superficiality, and pretension in modern society.
corrected_questions.passage:
Once upon a time, son,
they used to laugh with their hearts
and laugh with their eyes:
but now they only laugh with their teeth,
while their ice-block-cold eyes
search behind my shadow.

There was a time indeed
they used to shake hands with their hearts:
but that's gone, son.
Now they shake hands without hearts
while their left hands search
my empty pockets.

'Feel at home!' 'Come again':
they say, and when I come
again and feel
at home, once, twice,
there will be no thrice –
for then I find doors shut on me.

So I have learned many things, son.
I have learned to wear many faces
like dresses – homeface,
officeface, streetface, hostface,
cocktailface, with all their conforming smiles
like a fixed portrait smile.

And I have learned too
to laugh with only my teeth
and shake hands without my heart.
I have also learned to say, 'Goodbye',
when I mean 'Good-riddance':
to say 'Glad to meet you',
without being glad; and to say 'It's been
nice talking to you', after being bored.

But believe me, son.
I want to be what I used to be
when I was like you. I want
to unlearn all these muting things.
Most of all, I want to relearn
how to laugh, for my laugh in the mirror 
shows only my teeth like a snake's bare fangs!

So show me, son,
how to laugh; show me how
I used to laugh and smile
once upon a time when I was like you.

- Once Upon a Time, Gabriel Okara

corrected_questions.subject: Reading Comprehension
corrected_questions.question: As the word is used in line 14, "particular" describes the speaker's "remains" as all the following EXCEPT
corrected_questions.explanation: In the phrase 'particular remains', 'particular' means specific, individual, or personal (apart from others). While 'special rather than general' is a synonym for specific, 'precise' typically refers to exactness or accuracy, which is not the intended meaning in this context.
corrected_questions.passage:
(1) You see this gentle stream, that glides,
(2) Shoved on, by quick-succeeding tides:
(3) Try if this sober stream you can
(4) Follow to th' wilder ocean,
(5) And see, if there it keeps unspent
(6) In that congesting element.
(7) Next, from that world of waters, then
(8) By pores and caverns back again
(9) Induct that inadultrate same
(10) Stream to the spring from whence it came.
(11) This with a wonder when ye do,
(12) An easy, and else easier too:
(13) Then may ye collect the grains
(14) Of my particular remains,
(15) After a thousand lusters hurled,
(16) By ruffling winds, above the world.
 
— Proof to No Purpose, Robert Herrick (1591-1674)

corrected_questions.subject: Reading Comprehension
corrected_questions.question: Ang ipinahihiwatig ng pamagat ay
options: nagkalat ang mga huwad na bagay sa ating kapaligiran.
laganap ang pagbabalatkayo sa ating bansa.
laganap ang mapagkunwari sa iba't-ibang panig ng daigdig.
puro huwad ang makikita natin sa ating paligid.
corrected_questions.correctAnswer: laganap ang mapagkunwari sa iba't-ibang panig ng daigdig.
corrected_questions.explanation: Ang pamagat na 'May Plastik ang Mundo' ay gumagamit ng salitang 'plastik' bilang metapora para sa pagbabalatkayo o pagiging mapagkunwari. Dahil ang salitang ginamit ay 'Mundo', ang ipinahihiwatig nito ay ang paglaganap ng ganitong ugali sa buong daigdig, hindi lamang sa loob ng isang bansa.
corrected_questions.passage:
Matagal na, Huwan
matagal nang ipokrito ang laro mong ito
matagal nang ika'y may trono sa impyerno
at nilulumot na ang kapwa mo tao
sa iyo, lumang tugtugin na ang pag-ibig sa Diyos;
ang takot ay wala na't nilamon ng siglo,
ang ginto mong pader, malaya kay Kristo.

Natuwa ka sa iyong panalo
ang bahay mo'y pito, ang kotse ay walo
sa bahay-ampunan, sa "Mental Hospital",
kaharap-harap mo, kamera't potograpo.

Nagagalit kang madaya ng iba;
napopoot kang madaig sa kita
bago ang maskara'y suut-suot mo na
at ang simbaha'y ginawa mong bangketa.

Paluhud-luhod ka kung Linggo't Huwebes Santos,
Tapos duduran mo ang pulubi sa kanto
magmumura ka't manloloko ng tao
nasisikmura mo ang ganitong serbisyo?

Huwan, O Huwan!
Bakit? O bakit ba?
Ang magandang larawan ginawa mong dikdikan.
Dapat bang isigaw at ipagbulgaran...

- May Plastik ang Mundo, Odie Cruz Lacsamana
